_meta.description: Relevance taxonomy for life insurance product types. Used by the relevance checker to determine whether a query topic is applicable to the type of insurance product being discussed.
_meta.jurisdiction: SG (primary); principles broadly applicable to UK, US, AU
sources: MAS Notice 302 Product Development and Pricing
MAS Notice 320 Management of Participating Life Insurance Business
Life Insurance Association Singapore (LIA) Guidelines on Policy Illustrations
MoneySense.gov.sg: Understanding Endowment / Whole Life / Universal Life / ILP / Critical Illness / Disability Income Insurance
MoneySense.gov.sg: Investment-Linked Policies Guide to Fees and Pricing
LIA FAQ on Illustrated Investment Rate of Return (Jul 2021 revision: 4.25% / 3.00%)

termDefinitions.cash-value: The amount of money you receive if you surrender (cash in) a policy that has a savings feature. Builds up after the initial years. For ILPs, it equals the current market value of units held.
termDefinitions.surrender-value: The actual amount paid upon early termination of a policy; typically equals cash value minus any surrender charge or market value adjustment. Distinct from cash value in that surrender charges may apply in early years.
termDefinitions.dividends-bonuses: Non-guaranteed profit distributions from a participating fund. In Singapore, expressed as reversionary bonuses (declared annually, added to sum assured), terminal bonuses (paid at maturity/claim/surrender), and sometimes loyalty bonuses or cash dividends.
termDefinitions.reversionary-bonus: A non-guaranteed bonus declared annually by the insurer for participating policies; once declared it becomes a guaranteed addition to the policy value.
termDefinitions.terminal-bonus: A one-off non-guaranteed bonus payable at maturity, death claim, or surrender of a participating policy; represents a share of the participating fund's undistributed surplus.
termDefinitions.loyalty-bonus: An additional discretionary benefit some participating or endowment insurers credit at specific policy anniversaries (e.g., 10th, 20th year) as a reward for policy persistence. May be guaranteed or non-guaranteed depending on product.
termDefinitions.investment-sub-funds: Separately managed investment pools (unit trusts) within an ILP into which premiums are directed. The policyholder chooses among available sub-funds; each has its own stated investment objective.
termDefinitions.fund-switching: The ability to move all or part of the policy's unit holdings from one sub-fund to another within an ILP. Insurers typically allow a limited number of free switches per year.
termDefinitions.premium-holiday: A period during which the policyholder temporarily suspends premium payments while keeping the policy in force. In ILPs, the insurer redeems existing units to pay ongoing charges. In universal life, cash value absorbs charges. Not available in most term or non-par whole life products.
termDefinitions.no-claims-discount: A premium reduction reward for not making claims, typically associated with motor, travel, or health/shield insurance. Not a feature of traditional life, endowment, or disability income insurance products.
termDefinitions.waiting-period: A period from the policy start date (or diagnosis date) during which certain benefits are not payable. Most commonly applies to critical illness and disability income insurance. In CI: 30-90 days for most conditions. In DI: called the 'deferred period' or 'elimination period', typically 30-180 days before monthly benefits commence.
termDefinitions.survival-period: The period (usually 14-30 days) a claimant must survive after a critical illness diagnosis before the lump-sum benefit is paid. Distinct from waiting period.
termDefinitions.sum-assured: The guaranteed amount payable upon the insured event (death, TPD, or maturity). The face amount of the policy, which may be enhanced by bonuses in participating policies.
termDefinitions.sum-insured: Functionally equivalent to sum assured; the maximum benefit amount, often used in health/critical illness contexts.
termDefinitions.maturity-benefit: A lump-sum payout made at the end of the policy term if the insured is still alive. Applies to endowment policies (guaranteed + non-guaranteed portions) and some whole life policies with limited payment terms. Term and CI standalone policies have no maturity benefit.
termDefinitions.death-benefit: The amount paid to beneficiaries upon the insured's death. Applicable across virtually all life insurance product types. In ILPs, may be the higher of sum assured or fund value.
termDefinitions.policy-illustration: A document projecting future policy values under stated assumptions. For participating policies and universal life, MAS/LIA require two illustrative investment rates. For ILPs, illustrations use prescribed assumed rates of return. For non-par term, illustrations are simpler (no projection scenarios needed).
termDefinitions.projection-rates: The investment return assumptions used in policy illustrations. LIA-mandated cap rates for Singapore SGD par policies (as of Jul 2021): 4.25% p.a. (upper) and 3.00% p.a. (lower). ILP sub-fund illustrations use separate prescribed rates depending on fund type.
termDefinitions.participating-fund: The pool of assets managed by the insurer in which participating policyholders share profits through bonuses. Investments are mainly fixed income with some equities. Performance determines non-guaranteed bonus rates.
termDefinitions.guaranteed-benefits: Benefits contractually promised by the insurer regardless of investment performance, claims experience, or expenses.
termDefinitions.non-guaranteed-benefits: Benefits that depend on future experience (investment performance, claims, expenses) and are not contractually promised. Includes bonuses for par policies, and credited interest above the minimum for universal life.
termDefinitions.policy-loan: A loan facility allowing a policyholder to borrow against the policy's accumulated cash value, subject to interest. The loan reduces the effective cash value and death benefit until repaid. Only available where cash value exists.
termDefinitions.premium-payment-term: The period over which premiums must be paid (e.g., 5 years, 10 years, 20 years, or to age 65). Distinct from coverage term.
termDefinitions.coverage-term: The duration during which the policy provides protection coverage. For term life, this is a fixed period. For whole life and universal life, it is to death (whole of life). For endowment, it is a fixed savings/protection period.
termDefinitions.free-look-period: A mandatory cooling-off window (typically 14 days; 21 days for Shield Plans in Singapore) after policy issuance within which the policyholder may cancel and receive a full or partial premium refund. Required for all life insurance policies under MAS regulations.
termDefinitions.grace-period: A period (typically 30 days from the premium due date) during which a policyholder may pay an overdue premium without the policy lapsing. Applies across all life insurance product types.
termDefinitions.reinstatement: The process of restoring a lapsed policy to active status, subject to evidence of insurability, payment of overdue premiums (with interest), and any other insurer requirements. Applies to all life insurance product types.
termDefinitions.nomination: The policyholder's designation of beneficiary(ies) to receive the death benefit. In Singapore, governed by the Insurance (Nomination of Beneficiaries) Act. Applies across all life insurance product types.
termDefinitions.assignment: The legal transfer of ownership or beneficial interest in a policy from the policyholder (assignor) to another party (assignee), e.g., as collateral for a bank loan. Applies across most life insurance product types.
termDefinitions.surrender-charge: A fee levied on partial or full policy surrender before a specified holding period. Common in ILPs (especially in early years) and some universal life / endowment products. Reduces the net surrender value received.
termDefinitions.early-termination-charge: Equivalent to surrender charge; emphasises the cost of ending the policy before its intended term.
termDefinitions.bid-offer-spread: The difference between the price at which units are bought (offer price) and sold (bid price) in an ILP. Historically ~5% in Singapore. As of 23 September 2021, new ILP sub-funds must use single NAV-based pricing, eliminating the traditional spread for new policies.
termDefinitions.fund-management-charge: An annual fee charged by the sub-fund manager within an ILP, expressed as a percentage of the fund's net asset value; deducted before the unit price is published.
termDefinitions.mortality-charge: The cost of insurance (COI) deducted from an ILP's unit holdings or from universal life cash value to pay for the death benefit coverage. Increases with age.
termDefinitions.administration-charge: A fee covering policy administration costs; present in ILPs, universal life, and some par products. In ILPs, may also be called a 'policy fee'.
termDefinitions.accidental-death-benefit: An additional lump-sum benefit payable if death occurs as a direct result of an accident, typically equal to the base sum assured. Usually available as a rider on term life, whole life, and endowment policies. Not typically available as a standalone life insurance rider on CI or DI policies.
termDefinitions.retrenchment-benefit: A benefit providing a limited payout (or premium waiver) if the policyholder loses their job due to retrenchment. Offered as a rider or built-in feature in some Singapore term and whole life products.
termDefinitions.no-lapse-guarantee: A feature in some universal life policies guaranteeing that coverage will not lapse even if cash value falls to zero, provided a specified minimum premium is paid on time.
termDefinitions.return-of-premium: A benefit returning some or all premiums paid (without investment growth) if the insured survives to policy expiry (term life with ROP rider) or upon surrender (some endowment and whole life products). Not a standard feature; must be explicitly included.
termDefinitions.benefit-period: The maximum duration for which monthly disability income benefits are paid (e.g., 2 years, 5 years, to age 60/65). Specific to disability income insurance.
termDefinitions.elimination-period: The waiting (deferred) period in disability income insurance during which no benefit is paid, even if the insured is disabled. Policyholders self-insure this period. Shorter elimination periods result in higher premiums.
termDefinitions.monthly-benefit: The recurring payment amount paid to the insured under a disability income insurance policy. Typically up to 75-80% of pre-disability earned income.
termDefinitions.own-occupation-definition: A disability definition in DI insurance paying benefits if the insured cannot perform the duties of their specific occupation, even if they could work in another capacity.
termDefinitions.any-occupation-definition: A more restrictive disability definition paying benefits only if the insured cannot work in any occupation suited to their education, training, and experience.
termDefinitions.cost-of-living-adjustment-rider: A DI rider that increases monthly benefit payments annually (often linked to CPI) to maintain purchasing power during a long-term disability claim.
termDefinitions.premium-allocation-rate: The percentage of each ILP premium actually invested in sub-fund units (the remainder covering distribution costs). E.g., a 20% allocation rate means only 20 cents of each dollar buys units.
termDefinitions.crediting-rate: In universal life insurance, the interest rate declared by the insurer and credited to the cash value account. May be a declared fixed rate or linked to an index (IUL). Subject to a guaranteed minimum floor.
termDefinitions.index-account: In Indexed Universal Life (IUL), a cash value sub-account whose growth is linked to an external market index (e.g., S&P 500), subject to a floor (often 0%) and a cap rate.
termDefinitions.waiver-of-premium-rider: A rider that waives future premiums if the policyholder suffers a specified disability or critical illness, keeping the policy in force without further premium payments.
termDefinitions.tpd-rider: A rider paying all or part of the sum assured if the insured suffers Total and Permanent Disability.
termDefinitions.ci-rider: A rider accelerating or adding a lump-sum benefit upon diagnosis of a covered critical illness, attached to a base life insurance policy.
termDefinitions.paid-up-value: The reduced coverage amount a policy is converted to when the policyholder stops paying premiums but does not surrender; only available in policies with sufficient accumulated cash value.

term-life.label: Term Life Insurance
term-life.description: Pure protection cover for a fixed period. Pays a death benefit (and often TPD benefit) if the insured dies or becomes totally and permanently disabled within the policy term. No savings or investment element in the standard product.
relevant: return of premium (as optional rider only)
notApplicable: mortality charge (as a separately disclosed charge)
administration charge (as a separately disclosed charge)
benefit period (not applicable; coverage term applies instead)

conditionalNotes.surrender-charge: Standard term life has no surrender value and therefore no surrender charge. However, a term policy with a Return of Premium rider may have early termination implications.
conditionalNotes.policy-illustration: Policy illustrations for term life are simple; no investment projection scenarios are required since there is no savings component.
conditionalNotes.waiting-period: Not typical for term life death benefit. May apply to specific riders (e.g., a CI rider attached to term life may have a 90-day waiting period).

whole-life-participating.label: Whole Life Insurance (Participating)
whole-life-participating.description: Permanent life insurance with a savings element. Premiums are pooled into the insurer's participating fund; policyholders share in the fund's profits through non-guaranteed bonuses declared by the insurer on top of guaranteed benefits.
relevant: projection rates (4.25% and 3.00% p.a. for SGD par policies as of Jul 2021)
return of premium (built-in via cash value; explicit ROP rider less common)
premium holiday (some products, using cash value to fund premiums)
notApplicable: mortality charge (as a separately disclosed line item; embedded in premium)
administration charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
no-lapse guarantee (not typical for par whole life)
maturity benefit (no fixed maturity; pays on death or surrender)
waiting period (for death benefit; may apply to attached CI rider)

conditionalNotes.premium-holiday: Some par whole life products allow temporary suspension of premium payments if sufficient cash value has accumulated, but this is not a standard feature of all products.
conditionalNotes.maturity-benefit: Some limited-payment whole life policies may include a maturity value at age 100 or 120, but this is not a primary feature. Distinguished from endowment which has a defined maturity date.
conditionalNotes.return-of-premium: Not a separately stated rider in most par whole life; the cash value upon surrender serves a similar economic function.

whole-life-non-participating.label: Whole Life Insurance (Non-Participating)
whole-life-non-participating.description: Permanent life insurance with guaranteed benefits and guaranteed cash value growth. The policyholder does not share in the insurer's profits; all benefits (death benefit, cash value schedule) are fixed at policy inception.
relevant: policy illustration (simpler; shows guaranteed values only)
notApplicable: projection rates (no investment scenarios; all values guaranteed)
mortality charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
administration charge (as a separately disclosed line item)

endowment.label: Endowment Insurance
endowment.description: A hybrid product combining life insurance protection with a structured savings goal. Provides a death benefit if the insured dies during the policy term, and a maturity benefit (lump sum) if the insured survives to the end of the term. Most Singapore endowment plans are participating.
endowment.participatingStatus: both (participating is most common; non-participating endowments have guaranteed-only returns)
relevant: projection rates (4.25% and 3.00% p.a. for SGD par endowments)
coverage term (fixed savings horizon, e.g., 10, 15, 20 years)
return of premium (on early termination, partial return via surrender value)
notApplicable: mortality charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
administration charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
premium holiday (generally not available; some products may allow using cash value)
waiting period (for death benefit; may apply to CI rider)

conditionalNotes.dividends-bonuses: Only applicable to participating endowment plans. Non-participating endowments have no bonuses; all returns are guaranteed.
conditionalNotes.return-of-premium: Not a separate rider; if the insured survives to maturity, the maturity benefit (which includes premiums + investment return) is paid. Early surrender typically returns less than premiums paid in early years.

ilp.label: Investment-Linked Policy (ILP) / Variable Life Insurance
ilp.description: A bundled product combining life insurance protection with investment in unit trust sub-funds chosen by the policyholder. The cash value equals the market value of units held. The policyholder bears all investment risk. No guaranteed cash values in most ILPs.
ilp.participatingStatus: non-participating (investment risk borne entirely by policyholder)
relevant: death benefit (typically higher of sum assured or fund value, or sum assured plus fund value)
bid-offer spread (for policies issued before 23 Sep 2021; NAV pricing for newer policies)
mortality charge (cost of insurance; deducted by redeeming units)
projection rates (prescribed illustrative return rates for ILP sub-funds)
coverage term (can be whole of life for regular-premium ILPs)
notApplicable: guaranteed cash value (ILPs typically have no guaranteed cash value)
surrender value in the traditional guaranteed sense (value depends on market)
policy loan (ILPs generally do not offer policy loans; partial withdrawal is the equivalent)
non-guaranteed bonuses (ILP returns are directly market-linked; no bonus smoothing)
crediting rate (no declared interest rate; returns are market returns of sub-funds)
maturity benefit (most ILPs have no fixed maturity)

conditionalNotes.cash-value: ILPs do have a cash/fund value, but it is non-guaranteed and equals the market value of units. Questions about 'guaranteed cash value' do not apply.
conditionalNotes.bid-offer-spread: Applicable to older ILP policies. As of 23 September 2021, new Singapore ILP sub-funds must use single NAV-based pricing; bid-offer spread is eliminated for new policies.
conditionalNotes.policy-loan: Traditional policy loans are not available in ILPs. Partial withdrawals serve a similar function but permanently reduce the unit holdings.
conditionalNotes.projection-rates: ILP policy illustrations use prescribed return rate assumptions per MAS/LIA guidelines, but these are sub-fund performance projections, not insurer declared rates. Separate from par policy illustration rates.

universal-life.label: Universal Life Insurance (UL) / Indexed Universal Life (IUL)
universal-life.description: Flexible permanent life insurance with an unbundled structure. The policyholder pays flexible premiums; charges (mortality, administration) are transparently deducted from the cash value. The remaining cash value earns interest at a declared rate (UL) or an index-linked rate (IUL). Sold primarily for legacy/wealth transfer in Singapore. Policyholders are NOT entitled to a share of the insurer's profits.
relevant: crediting rate (declared by insurer; variable, above a guaranteed minimum floor)
guaranteed minimum crediting rate (floor; often 0% for IUL index account, e.g., 1-2% for UL fixed account)
cap rate (IUL: maximum credited rate in an index segment period)
floor rate (IUL: minimum credited rate, protecting against negative index returns)
mortality charge (cost of insurance; deducted monthly from cash value)
premium flexibility (ability to vary premium within policy limits)
premium holiday (ability to skip premiums if cash value is sufficient to cover charges)
notApplicable: investment sub-funds (UL/IUL is not an ILP; policyholder does not choose sub-funds)
fund switching (no sub-funds; though IUL allows allocation between fixed and index accounts)
fund management charge (cost structure is different; mortality + admin charges instead)
maturity benefit (no fixed maturity; coverage is whole of life)

conditionalNotes.fund-switching: Not applicable in the ILP sense. However, IUL policyholders can reallocate their cash value between the fixed account and index account(s), which is sometimes informally called switching.
conditionalNotes.projection-rates: Policy illustrations for UL/IUL show projected values under stated credited interest rate scenarios, which is analogous to but distinct from the LIA par policy illustration rates.
conditionalNotes.no-lapse-guarantee: Only available in specific UL product designs (NLG UL). Must be confirmed with the product's policy contract.
conditionalNotes.crediting-rate: For traditional UL, the insurer declares a crediting rate periodically. For IUL, the rate is determined by index performance subject to floor and cap. Neither is guaranteed beyond the minimum floor.

critical-illness-standalone.label: Critical Illness Insurance (Standalone)
critical-illness-standalone.description: A standalone policy (not a rider) paying a lump-sum benefit upon diagnosis of a covered critical illness condition. In Singapore, LIA standards define 37 severe-stage critical illnesses. The policy ends after the lump sum is paid. No savings, investment, or monthly income component in the standard product.
relevant: sum assured / sum insured (the lump sum CI benefit amount)
critical illness definitions (LIA standard list: 37 severe-stage conditions)
waiting period (typically 30-90 days from policy start; no benefit if CI diagnosed during waiting period)
survival period (typically 14-30 days post-diagnosis; must survive this period to receive payout)
early-stage CI coverage (some products cover early and intermediate stages at reduced benefit)
multi-pay CI (some products allow multiple claims for different conditions)
waiver of premium rider (some products waive CI premiums on first CI claim)
policy lapse on payout (most standalone CI policies terminate after full payout)
notApplicable: mortality charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
administration charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
benefit period (CI pays a one-off lump sum; no recurring benefit period)
elimination period / deferred period (DI concept; CI uses 'waiting period' and 'survival period')
death benefit (standalone CI does not automatically pay a death benefit; a separate life policy is needed)

conditionalNotes.death-benefit: Some CI standalone policies include a death benefit (returning premiums or paying a small sum on death), but this is an added feature, not a standard characteristic. The core benefit is the CI lump sum.
conditionalNotes.return-of-premium: Some CI policies offer a return of premium upon death or policy expiry if no CI claim was made. Must be confirmed with the specific product.
conditionalNotes.waiting-period-vs-survival-period: These are two distinct CI concepts: the waiting period starts from policy inception (no benefit if diagnosed during this window); the survival period starts from the date of diagnosis (must survive this many days to receive the lump sum).
conditionalNotes.tpd: Many standalone CI policies do not cover TPD; TPD is typically a separate rider or is covered under a separate life/disability policy.

disability-income.label: Disability Income Insurance (DI)
disability-income.description: A policy paying a regular monthly income benefit to replace lost earnings if the insured becomes disabled and unable to work. Benefits are paid for a defined benefit period after the elimination period has been served. Pure income-replacement insurance; no savings, investment, or lump-sum element in the standard product.
relevant: monthly benefit (the income replacement amount; typically up to 75-80% of pre-disability income)
benefit period (maximum duration of monthly benefit payments; e.g., 2 years, 5 years, to age 60/65)
elimination period / deferred period / waiting period (time insured must be continuously disabled before benefits commence; typically 30-180 days)
disability definition: own occupation vs any occupation
waiver of premium (typically built-in; premiums waived while on claim)
retrenchment benefit (some Singapore DI products include this as a separate trigger)
non-cancelable policy (premiums and terms locked in)
notApplicable: death benefit (standalone DI does not pay a death benefit; separate life policy needed)
sum assured / sum insured (DI uses 'monthly benefit', not a lump-sum sum assured)
mortality charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
administration charge (as a separately disclosed line item)
ci rider (as a DI feature; CI is separate)
survival period (CI concept; not used in DI)
lump sum payout (DI pays monthly; any lump-sum element is a separate rider)

conditionalNotes.waiting-period-vs-elimination-period: In DI insurance, the 'waiting period' and 'elimination period' or 'deferred period' are the same concept: the initial period of disability that must pass before monthly benefits begin. This is distinct from the CI 'waiting period' (which starts from policy inception, not from the onset of disability).
conditionalNotes.sum-assured: DI insurance does not use 'sum assured' as the primary benefit metric. The relevant metric is the monthly benefit amount. Asking about sum assured in the context of a DI policy would be a relevance mismatch.
conditionalNotes.retrenchment-benefit: Some Singapore DI products (e.g., Income Insurance's policy) include a retrenchment benefit as an additional trigger. This is a product-specific add-on, not a standard DI feature.
conditionalNotes.tpd: DI covers ongoing inability to work due to disability, not permanent disability per se. TPD is a separate life insurance concept. However, some DI policies may include a TPD waiver.
conditionalNotes.death-benefit: Some DI products include a small accidental death or dismemberment benefit as a rider, but the core product does not provide a life insurance death benefit.

crossProductTermMatrix._description: Quick-reference matrix showing which terms apply (Y), do not apply (N), conditionally apply (C), or are core/primary features (*) for each product type.

surrender-value.ilp: C (fund value minus surrender charge; non-guaranteed)

fund-switching.universal-life: C (reallocation between fixed/index accounts in IUL)

no-claims-discount._note: No-claims discount is a motor/health insurance concept; does not apply to any life insurance product type.

waiting-period.term-life: N (for death benefit; C for attached CI rider)
waiting-period.whole-life-par: N (for death benefit; C for attached CI rider)
waiting-period.whole-life-non-par: N (for death benefit; C for attached CI rider)
waiting-period.endowment: N (for death benefit; C for attached CI rider)
waiting-period.ilp: N (for death benefit; C for attached CI rider)
waiting-period.universal-life: N (for death benefit; C for attached CI rider)
waiting-period.disability-income: Y* (called elimination period or deferred period)

maturity-benefit.whole-life-par: N (no fixed maturity; pays on death or surrender)
maturity-benefit.ilp: N (no fixed maturity for regular-premium ILPs)

death-benefit.critical-illness-standalone: C (some products; not the core benefit)

projection-rates.whole-life-non-par: N (all values guaranteed; no projection scenarios)

bid-offer-spread.ilp: C (applies to pre-23 Sep 2021 policies; eliminated for new policies)

mortality-charge.term-life: N (embedded in premium; not disclosed separately)

return-of-premium.whole-life-par: C (economically via cash value; explicit ROP rider uncommon)
return-of-premium.whole-life-non-par: C (via guaranteed cash value on surrender)
return-of-premium.endowment: Y (core concept: maturity benefit returns capital + return)
return-of-premium.ilp: N (fund value may be less than premiums paid)
return-of-premium.critical-illness-standalone: C (some products return premiums if no claim; product-specific)

retrenchment-benefit.term-life: C (product-specific; available in some Singapore term plans)

surrender-charge.endowment: Y (early termination yields less than premiums paid)

participating-vs-non-participating.endowment: Y (can be either; most SG endowments are par)
participating-vs-non-participating.ilp: N (not a par product; investment risk with policyholder)
participating-vs-non-participating.universal-life: N (non-par; not entitled to insurer profits)

relevanceCheckRules._description: Heuristic rules for the relevance checker to flag likely mismatches between a query topic and the product type context.

rules.message: This concept applies to savings-type insurance products (whole life, endowment, universal life). The product you are asking about does not accumulate cash value.
Bonuses and dividends are features of participating (par) insurance products only. This product type is non-participating and does not declare bonuses.
Sub-funds, fund switching, and unit-pricing concepts are specific to Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs). This product type does not have investment sub-funds.

Separately disclosed mortality and administration charges are specific to ILPs and universal life products. In traditional products, these costs are embedded in the premium and not disclosed as line items.
Maturity benefits are a defining feature of endowment insurance. This product type does not have a fixed maturity date with a savings payout.
Monthly income benefits, benefit periods, and elimination periods are concepts specific to Disability Income Insurance. This product type does not provide ongoing monthly income replacement.

Waiting periods (from policy inception before CI is covered) and survival periods (post-diagnosis survival requirement) are concepts specific to Critical Illness insurance. In Disability Income insurance, the equivalent concept is called the 'elimination period' or 'deferred period', which starts from the onset of disability — not from policy inception.
rules.note: When a user mentions 'waiting period' in the context of disability income insurance, clarify that this may refer to the elimination/deferred period.

rules.message: No-claims discounts (NCD) are a feature of motor insurance (and some health/shield plans). Life insurance, critical illness, and disability income policies do not offer NCD.
No-lapse guarantees are a feature of specific Universal Life product designs. This product type does not have a no-lapse guarantee mechanism.
Crediting rates, index accounts, and associated rate caps/floors are concepts specific to Universal Life and Indexed Universal Life products.
LIA-mandated policy illustration projection rates apply to participating policies and ILPs. Non-participating products with no investment element do not require multi-scenario investment projections.

Disability Income Insurance does not use 'sum assured' as its primary benefit measure. The relevant concept is the monthly benefit amount (the monthly income replacement). Consider whether you meant to ask about monthly benefit instead.

Premium holidays (temporarily suspending premium payments while keeping the policy active) are primarily a feature of ILPs and Universal Life policies, and some participating whole life products. This product type generally does not offer premium holidays.
